# Kuzhiyundo

> Community-driven pothole tracking map for Kerala, India.

Kuzhiyundo (meaning "Is there a pothole?" in Malayalam) is an open-source platform where residents can report, upvote, and track road potholes across Kerala. Reports include severity, location, constituency info, and road authority classification.

## Features

- Interactive map showing community-reported potholes across Kerala
- Report a pothole by tracing a road segment on the map
- Severity classification: low, medium, high
- Road authority tagging: Ward Member, Panchayat/LSGD, MLA/State PWD, MP/NHAI
- Constituency lookup: Assembly, Parliamentary, and LSGD boundaries
- Community voting to confirm or dispute reports
- Photo uploads for visual evidence

## Data

- Reports are geolocated with encoded polyline paths
- Each report includes address, district, pincode, AC/PC/LSGD constituency
- Road authority derived from OpenStreetMap highway tags via Overpass API
- Real-time updates via Firebase Firestore

## Links

- App: https://kuzhiyundo.com
- Source: https://github.com/sajithlaldev/kuzhiyundo
